data.title: Rakuten GAAP EPS of -¥34.09, revenue of ¥562.7B; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Rakuten press release (OTCPK:RKUNY): Q1 GAAP EPS of -¥34.09.
  • Revenue of ¥562.7B (+9.6% Y/Y).
  • Estimate of Consolidated Operating Results for the fiscal year 2025 (January 1 to December 31, 2025) For the estimate of consolidated operating results for the fiscal year 2025, we aim for double-digit growth in consolidated revenue compared to the fiscal year 2024, excluding the securities business whose results are heavily impacted by stock market conditions.

data.title: Starbucks baristas strike at more than 50 stores due to new dress code
data.description:
Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) baristas across the United States have intensified their labor actions in 2025, staging walkouts and strikes over unresolved contract negotiations and workplace policies. The latest wave of protests was triggered by the coffee chain giant's new dress code policy, which requires employees to wear solid black tops and blue, black, or khaki bottoms. Many baristas argue this policy imposes an unfair financial burden, as they are only provided with two company shirts and must purchase additional clothing themselves if they work multiple shifts. 

Starbucks Workers United, the union representing over 11,000 workers at more than 570 stores, says the walkouts have occurred at more than 50 U.S. "Workers shouldn't need to spend $$$ out-of-pocket to replace perfectly good shirts, pants, & shoes when we’re already struggling to get by," read a social media post from the union.

In its last update, Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) said that there has been no significant impact on store operations on a national level and most stores are open and serving customers as usual.

Beyond the dress code dispute, contract talks between Starbucks (SBUX) and its unionized workers have stalled for over two years. The union recently rejected the company's latest proposal, which included annual raises of at least 2%, saying it fell short on wages, healthcare, and scheduling guarantees. Workers are demanding a $20 per hour minimum wage, 5% annual raises, better healthcare, predictable schedules, and improved paid leave. For its part, Starbucks (SBUX) maintains that its pay and benefits are competitive, stating that average hourly compensation, including benefits, is about $30 for most baristas.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) traded flat in the premarket session on Wednesday after gaining 1.4% on Tuesday. The coffee chain stock is down 4.8% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: DeFi Technologies reports Q1 results
data.description:
• DeFi Technologies press release (NASDAQ:DEFT): Q1 revenues of C$62.7 million (approximately US$43.1 million) for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to C$4.9 million in Q1 2024.  Revenues increased due to increased staking income and management fees due to higher AUM and an increase in digital asset prices.
  • Net Income of C$43.0 million (approximately US$30.0 million) in Q1 2025, up 711% from a loss of C$19.3 million in Q1 2024. The positive net income reflects the critical scale achieved by the Company.

data.title: Bruker and 10x Genomics reach global settlement of patent litigation
data.description:
Bruker (NASDAQ:BRKR) and 10x Genomics (NASDAQ:TXG) have reached a final settlement to resolve their patent disputes and dismiss multiple litigations, with global patent cross-license agreements between the two companies. 

In connection with the settlement, all ongoing lawsuits and administrative proceedings filed by both companies in several countries, including actions pending in the United States, in Germany, and before the European Unified Patent Court, will be withdrawn, the companies said.

“We’re pleased that we have reached a worldwide settlement with 10x, putting an end to all litigation between us,” said Mark R. Munch, President of the Bruker Nano Group. 

“This is a great outcome for the scientific community, who can continue their work using Bruker’s CosMx® and GeoMx® spatial biology platforms without concern that litigation could interfere with their advancements in oncology, neurobiology, and other fields of research."

data.title: TELUS Health inks strategic partnership with GTCR as part of Workplace Options deal
data.description:
• Telus (NYSE:TU) Wednesday announced a strategic partnership with GTCR, supporting TELUS Health's recently announced acquisition of Workplace Options.
  • GTCR to invest $200 million as part of the acquisition, bringing strategic healthcare industry expertise to accelerate TELUS Health's global growth and innovation.

data.title: Nucor stops some production after cybersecurity incident
data.description:
Nucor (NYSE:NUE) said Wednesday it has stopped some production at various steel facilities after identifying a cybersecurity incident that involved unauthorized third party access to certain information technology systems used by the company.

The steelmaker said in an SEC filing that it is in the process of restarting the affected operations as it investigates the incident with the assistance of external cybersecurity experts, and it has notified federal law enforcement authorities.

Nucor (NYSE:NUE) said it is taking steps to contain and respond to the incident, including proactively taking potentially affected systems offline and implementing other containment, remediation, or recovery measures.

data.title: Saudi Arabia AI deals were 'bullish eye opener' for tech investors: Wedbush
data.description:
The number of artificial intelligence-focused deals signed yesterday between U.S. tech companies and Saudi Arabia is a “bullish eye-opener” for investors, with the prospects of more to come, Wedbush Securities said.

“The theme was clear in Riyadh... the AI Revolution is coming to the Saudi Kingdom and Riyadh will be a major buyer of AI chips, software, autonomous/robotics, and datacenters over the next decade,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients.

They see the deals — which include those signed by Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), AMD (NASDAQ:AMD), Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO) and others — as a “huge opportunity” to expand the total addressable market for all things AI.

“We believe the market opportunity in Saudi Arabia could over time add another $1 trillion to the broader global AI market in the coming years and this dynamic is not being priced into the market and tech names in our view,” the analysts explained.

And while the market may have reacted positively to the U.S. and China ratcheting down the trade war, China still does not have access to the latest AI accelerators, whereas Saudi Arabia does, notably Nvidia's, the analysts pointed out.

“This will be just the beginning as tech players such as the hyperscalers, Palantir, Tesla and others see the game changing AI market opportunity in the Middle East and we expect bigger AI deals on the horizon.”

data.title: European markets look for direction as uncertainty over trade lingers
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET

London (UKX) +0.04% to 8,607. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.47% to 23,527. Germany's inflation drops to 2.1% in April

France (CAC:IND) -0.59% to 7,827.

Spain's inflation was recorded at 2.2% in April.

Slovakia’s annual inflation rate slowed to 3.7% in April.

Hungary's construction output shrank by 3.4% Y/Y in March.

Sweden’s annual inflation rate eased to 0.3% in April.

The annual inflation rate in Finland held steady at 0.5% in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.12% lower to approximately 544.5, fluctuating around the flat line. The markets have shown signs of losing steam, as investor sentiment turned cautious amid uncertainty over the specifics and enforcement of global trade agreements. On the corporate front, Burberry Group (OTCPK:BURBY) jumped following the announcement of several organizational changes.

Coming up in the next session: A lighter day on the European economic calendar today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield down 5 basis points at 4.45%.

Germany's 10-year yield was dwn 1 basis point to 2.67%.

UK's 10-year yield was flat at 4.67%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
